test.command: npm run test:contract:all && node --test tests/*.test.mjs && npm run test:browser-use-discovery

typecheck.command: node --check app.js && node --check dev-server.mjs && node --check discovery-wizard-local.js && node --check discovery-wizard-probes.js && node --check discovery-wizard-relay.js && node --check discovery-wizard-shell.js && node --check discovery-wizard-verify.js && node --check settings-tabs.js && node --check user-content-store.js && node --check resume-bundle.js && node --check resume-generate.js && node --check document-templates.js && node --check server/index.mjs && node --check server/job-scraper.mjs && node --check server/ats-scorecard.mjs

blockingIssues.description: The shared install surface is not reusable after first bootstrap because `.factory/init.sh` only runs `npm install` when dependency directories are missing, so later dependency changes make the manifest install command a no-op.
Live ATS requests remain off-contract because `app.js` always includes `providerHint`, but the published v1 ATS request schema disallows that field.
Discovery readiness/preflight still trusts any non-empty Sheets credential setting, so broken credential-file paths or malformed JSON can be reported as ready until the later write path fails.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented that `integrations/browser-use-discovery/state/worker-config.json` is a checked-in non-secret seed config tied to the disposable validation sheet.
Expanded the shared contracts map to include the discovery async acknowledgement/status surfaces (`statusPath`, `pollAfterMs`, `/runs/{runId}`).
Recorded that `npm run test:ats-contract` currently validates ATS example fixtures only, so live ATS request builders still require direct review when request shape changes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update milestone starter-sheet expectations from columns A–S to the current A–T contract so mission validation artifacts match the `Logo URL` header now used by README, app.js, and the pipeline schema.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `pipeline-contract-and-starter-sheet-alignment` found mission validation docs still describing starter-sheet creation through column `S` while the reviewed implementation and repo contract use column `T` (`Logo URL`).

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a dirty-baseline execution path that explains how to produce isolated commits when AGENTS.md declares the working tree intentionally dirty, and suppress any generic clean-git-status requirement in that case.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both `pipeline-contract-and-starter-sheet-alignment` and `ats-server-and-scorecard-readiness` reported skill deviations because mission AGENTS guidance requires preserving a dirty baseline while the worker procedure still expects clean git status.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Explicitly allow curl plus seeded sheet readback as valid end-to-end evidence for worker-only discovery features when there is no browser-side UI signal to verify.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `discovery-worker-readiness-and-write-contract` found the worker had to record a skill deviation even though curl responses plus sheet readback were the correct contract evidence for append/update and async-completion behavior.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Once the non-secret starter-sheet browser bootstrap surface is present in the main worktree, document it as the default no-mock path for starter-sheet setup validation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for `pipeline-contract-and-starter-sheet-alignment` and `repo-validation-surface-hardening` both reported that starter-sheet verification lacked shared no-secret bootstrap guidance and therefore required mock-based or ad hoc validation workflows.

rejectedObservations.observation: The shared testing docs should be updated immediately to reference the reviewed starter-sheet browser bootstrap surface.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous: the reviewed commit referenced `validation/starter-sheet-browser-bootstrap.html` and a `starter_sheet_browser_bootstrap` service entry, but those artifacts are not present in the current worktree, so documenting them now would risk codifying a shared surface that is not actually available here.
